<style>
  :root{
    --bg:#0f1218; --panel:#161b24; --panel2:#1c2230;
    --ink:#e8ecf1; --mute:#9aa4b2; --line:#2a3242;
    --yellow:#ffd866; --blue:#58c4dd; --green:#83c167;
    --pink:#e07a5f; --violet:#b39ddb; --cyan:#7de0d6;
  }
  *{box-sizing:border-box}
  html,body{margin:0;padding:0;background:var(--bg);color:var(--ink);

  p{margin:0.7rem 0}

    padding:1rem 1.1rem 1.2rem;margin:1.2rem 0}
  .widget .hd{display:flex;justify-content:space-between;align-items:baseline;margin-bottom:.6rem;gap:.8rem;flex-wrap:wrap}
  .widget .hd .ttl{font-weight:600;color:#fff;font-size:.95rem;letter-spacing:.02em;text-transform:uppercase}
  .widget .hd .hint{color:var(--mute);font-size:.85rem}

  .row label{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem}
  input[type="text"],input[type="number"],select{
    background:#0b0f16;color:var(--ink);border:1px solid var(--line);

  button:hover{background:#242c3e}
  button.active{background:var(--yellow);color:#111;border-color:var(--yellow)}

    min-height:1.6em;white-space:pre-wrap;word-break:break-word}
  .pill{display:inline-block;background:var(--panel2);border:1px solid var(--line);
    border-radius:999px;padding:1px 8px;font-size:.82rem;color:var(--mute);margin-right:4px}
  .note{border-left:3px solid var(--violet);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d8d2ef;background:#1a1828;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .ok{border-left:3px solid var(--green);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#d5ebc8;background:#1a2418;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  .bad{border-left:3px solid var(--pink);padding:.3rem .9rem;color:#f4d2c7;background:#2a1a18;
    margin:1rem 0;border-radius:0 6px 6px 0}
  svg{display:block;margin:0 auto;max-width:100%}
  svg text{fill:var(--ink);font-family:inherit}
  nav.toc{position:sticky;top:0;background:rgba(15,18,24,.93);backdrop-filter:blur(8px);
    border-bottom:1px solid var(--line);padding:.6rem 1rem;z-index:10;
    display:flex;gap:1rem;flex-wrap:wrap;font-size:.9rem}
  nav.toc a{color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none}
  nav.toc a:hover{color:var(--yellow)}
  /* sticky sidebar TOC (wide screens only; narrow screens fall back to top-nav) */
  aside.sidetoc{position:fixed;top:4.2rem;
    left:max(1rem, calc(50% - 440px - 220px));
    width:200px;max-height:calc(100vh - 6rem);overflow-y:auto;
    padding-right:1rem;border-right:1px solid var(--line);
    font-size:.88rem;z-index:5}
  aside.sidetoc .sidetoc-title{font-size:.7rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute);margin:0 0 .5rem;padding-bottom:.35rem;border-bottom:1px solid var(--line)}
  aside.sidetoc ol{list-style:none;padding:0;margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc li{margin:0}
  aside.sidetoc a{display:block;padding:.3rem .5rem;color:var(--mute);text-decoration:none;
    border-left:2px solid transparent;line-height:1.35;
    transition:color 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease}
  aside.sidetoc a:hover{color:var(--ink)}
  aside.sidetoc a.active{color:var(--yellow);border-left-color:var(--yellow)}
  @media (max-width:1180px){ aside.sidetoc{display:none} }
  @media (min-width:1181px){ nav.toc a[href^="#"]{display:none} }

  .small{font-size:.88rem;color:var(--mute)}
  footer{color:var(--mute);font-size:.88rem;text-align:center;margin-top:4rem}
  table.plain{width:100%;border-collapse:collapse;font-size:.95rem;margin:.6rem 0}

  table.plain th{color:var(--mute);font-weight:500}
  /* callback-css-auto-begin */
  aside.callback{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:rgba(88,196,221,0.05);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--cyan) 45%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.callback .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;

  }
  aside.callback ul{margin:0;padding-left:1.1rem}
  aside.callback ul li{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.callback a{color:inherit}
/* callback-css-auto-end */
  details.changelog{
    margin:2rem 0 1rem;padding:.6rem .9rem;
    background:rgba(0,0,0,0.22);
    border-radius:6px;
    font-size:.85rem;
  }
  details.changelog summary{
    cursor:pointer;letter-spacing:.1em;text-transform:uppercase;
    font-size:.7rem;color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);
  }
  details.changelog table{margin-top:.5rem;border-collapse:collapse;width:100%;font-size:.82rem}
  details.changelog td{padding:.2rem .6rem;border-bottom:1px dashed rgba(255,255,255,0.07);vertical-align:top}
  details.changelog td:first-child{color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);white-space:nowrap;font-variant-numeric:tabular-nums}
  aside.related{
    margin:1.2rem 0;padding:.7rem 1rem;
    background:color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 6%, transparent);
    border-left:3px solid color-mix(in srgb, var(--mute) 55%, transparent);
    border-radius:0 6px 6px 0;
    font-size:.93rem;
  }
  aside.related .ttl{
    font-size:.68rem;letter-spacing:.14em;text-transform:uppercase;
    color:var(--mute,#8c9aa6);margin-bottom:.3rem;font-weight:600;
  }
  aside.related > div{margin:.15rem 0}
  aside.related .more{color:var(--mute);font-style:italic}
  aside.related a{color:inherit}
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-begin */
  html[data-hide-widgets] .widget { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-quizzes] .quiz { display: none !important; }
  html[data-hide-lineage] .lineage-strip { display: none !important; }
  .mv-display-toggles { display: inline-flex; gap: .6rem; align-items: center; }
  .mv-display-toggle { background: transparent; border: 1px solid var(--line, #333); border-radius: 6px; padding: 5px 10px; cursor: pointer; font-size: 1.05rem; line-height: 1; color: inherit; transition: background 120ms ease, border-color 120ms ease; }
  .mv-display-toggle:hover { background: var(--panel, rgba(255,255,255,0.06)); border-color: var(--mute, #666); }
  .mv-display-toggle--off { opacity: 0.35; }
  /* display-prefs-css-auto-end */

Group cohomology

Derived $G$-fixed points: a single machine that controls extensions, twisted forms, Hilbert 90, and Brauer groups.

Let $G$ be a group and $M$ a (left) $G$-module — i.e. an abelian group on which $G$ acts. The functor $M\mapsto M^G=\{m\in M:g\cdot m=m\;\forall g\}$ of fixed points is left exact but not right exact: a surjection $M\twoheadrightarrow N$ of $G$-modules need not surject on fixed points (an element of $N^G$ may lift to $M$ only after twisting by a cocycle). The right derived functors $H^n(G,-)=R^n(-)^G$ are group cohomology. Equivalently — and this is the working definition — the cohomology of the complex of cochains on the bar resolution of $\mathbb{Z}$ over $\mathbb{Z}[G]$.

Three things make this notebook entry unusually fertile. First, the low-dimensional groups have crisp interpretations: $H^0=M^G$, $H^1$ classifies twisted forms / crossed homomorphisms, $H^2$ classifies extensions. Second, when $G=\mathrm{Gal}(\bar k/k)$ acts on a Galois module, group cohomology becomes Galois cohomology, and the punch lines are Hilbert 90 and the Brauer group. Third, for cyclic $G$ the cohomology is periodic with period 2 — a genuine miracle that powers class field theory.

blocks.html:
1. The bar resolution and $H^n(G,M)$

To compute $\mathrm{Ext}^n_{\mathbb{Z}[G]}(\mathbb{Z},M)$ we need a free (or projective) resolution of the trivial $G$-module $\mathbb{Z}$. The bar resolution is universal and explicit:

$$\cdots\;\xrightarrow{\,d_3\,}\;\mathbb{Z}[G]^{\otimes 3}\;\xrightarrow{\,d_2\,}\;\mathbb{Z}[G]^{\otimes 2}\;\xrightarrow{\,d_1\,}\;\mathbb{Z}[G]\;\xrightarrow{\,\varepsilon\,}\;\mathbb{Z}\;\to\;0,$$

where $\varepsilon(\sum n_g g)=\sum n_g$ is the augmentation, and a typical generator $[g_1|g_2|\dots|g_n]$ in degree $n$ has differential

$$d[g_1|\dots|g_n]\;=\;g_1[g_2|\dots|g_n]\;+\;\sum_{i=1}^{n-1}(-1)^i[g_1|\dots|g_ig_{i+1}|\dots|g_n]\;+\;(-1)^n[g_1|\dots|g_{n-1}].$$

Apply $\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathbb{Z}[G]}(-,M)$. An $n$-cochain is then a function $\varphi:G^n\to M$ (with $\varphi=m\in M$ when $n=0$), and the coboundary becomes

$$(d\varphi)(g_1,\dots,g_{n+1})\;=\;g_1\cdot\varphi(g_2,\dots,g_{n+1})\;+\;\sum_{i=1}^{n}(-1)^i\varphi(\dots,g_ig_{i+1},\dots)\;+\;(-1)^{n+1}\varphi(g_1,\dots,g_n).$$

The cohomology of $(\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathbb{Z}[G]}(\mathbb{Z}[G]^{\otimes\bullet+1},M),d^*)$ is $H^n(G,M)$. Different resolutions of $\mathbb{Z}$ (free $\mathbb{Z}[G]$-resolutions are non-unique) give canonically isomorphic cohomology — that is the whole point of derived functors.

Coboundary calculator
type a cochain, get $d\varphi$
choose a degree and fill in values…
A $1$-cocycle obeys $\varphi(gh)=\varphi(g)+g\cdot\varphi(h)$. A $2$-cocycle obeys $g_1\varphi(g_2,g_3)-\varphi(g_1g_2,g_3)+\varphi(g_1,g_2g_3)-\varphi(g_1,g_2)=0$.

The bar resolution is conceptually clean but enormous. In practice one uses smaller resolutions whenever they are available — see §5 for the periodic resolution that handles cyclic groups in two terms.

See also
  • Group actions, orbits, and Burnside
  • Derived functors and Ext/Tor

Used in
Group cohomology · $H^0$ as $G$-fixed points
</section>

2. $H^0(G,M)=M^G$

The degree-0 cohomology is exactly the fixed points:

$$H^0(G,M)\;=\;M^G\;=\;\{\,m\in M\;:\;g\cdot m=m\;\forall g\in G\,\}.$$

Indeed a $0$-cochain is just an element $m\in M$, and the coboundary $(dm)(g)=g\cdot m-m$ vanishes precisely when $m$ is fixed.

Although it sits at the bottom of the tower, $H^0$ is doing the only thing the higher groups exist to repair. The functor $(-)^G:\mathsf{Mod}_{\mathbb{Z}[G]}\to\mathsf{Ab}$ is left exact: applying it to a short exact sequence $$0\;\to\;A\;\to\;B\;\to\;C\;\to\;0$$ of $G$-modules gives an exact sequence $$0\;\to\;A^G\;\to\;B^G\;\to\;C^G$$ but the map $B^G\to C^G$ need not be surjective. The obstruction lives in $H^1$, and the long exact sequence of cohomology $$0\to A^G\to B^G\to C^G\xrightarrow{\delta} H^1(G,A)\to H^1(G,B)\to H^1(G,C)\xrightarrow{\delta}H^2(G,A)\to\cdots$$ is the central computational tool of the entire subject.

m=6
…
$M^G=\ker(\sigma-1)$. For $\sigma\cdot x=k\cdot x$ on $\mathbb{Z}/m$, fixed points are multiples of $m/\gcd(k-1,m)$. Trivial action ($k=1$) gives $M^G=M$; sign action on $\mathbb{Z}/m$ ($k=-1$) gives the $2$-torsion.

Used in
Group cohomology · $H^1$, crossed homomorphisms, and Hilbert 90
</section>

3. $H^1$: crossed homomorphisms and Hilbert 90

Untangling the bar formulas in degree 1: a $1$-cocycle (also called a crossed homomorphism or derivation) is a function $\varphi:G\to M$ satisfying the twisted homomorphism identity

$$\varphi(gh)\;=\;\varphi(g)\;+\;g\cdot\varphi(h).$$

If $G$ acts trivially this collapses to the ordinary homomorphism law. A $1$-coboundary is one of the form $\varphi(g)=g\cdot m-m$ for some $m\in M$. So

$$H^1(G,M)\;=\;\frac{\{\text{crossed homomorphisms }G\to M\}}{\{\text{principal: }g\mapsto gm-m\}}.$$

When the action is trivial, every $1$-cocycle is a homomorphism and every coboundary is zero, so $H^1(G,M)=\mathrm{Hom}(G,M)$. When the action is nontrivial, things twist.

Hilbert's Theorem 90 (cyclic version). Let $L/K$ be a cyclic extension of degree $n$ with $G=\mathrm{Gal}(L/K)=\langle\sigma\rangle$, and let $L^\times$ be the multiplicative group of $L$, viewed as a $G$-module. Then $$H^1(G,L^\times)\;=\;1.$$ Equivalently: every element $\beta\in L^\times$ with $N_{L/K}(\beta)=1$ has the form $\beta=\sigma(\alpha)/\alpha$ for some $\alpha\in L^\times$.

The proof is short and instructive. A $1$-cocycle $\varphi:G\to L^\times$ is determined by $\beta=\varphi(\sigma)\in L^\times$ (using the cocycle identity to extend), and the cocycle identity in cyclic form is exactly $N_{L/K}(\beta)=\beta\cdot\sigma\beta\cdots\sigma^{n-1}\beta=1$. To exhibit a coboundary $\beta=\sigma(\alpha)/\alpha$, set $$\alpha\;=\;\sum_{i=0}^{n-1}\beta\cdot\sigma\beta\cdots\sigma^{i-1}\beta\cdot\sigma^i(c)$$ for a $c\in L^\times$ chosen so the sum is nonzero (possible by linear independence of characters). Then $\sigma(\alpha)=\beta^{-1}\alpha$ — done.

Hilbert 90 in $\mathbb{Q}(i)/\mathbb{Q}$
factor norm-one $\beta\in\mathbb{Q}(i)^\times$ as $\sigma(\alpha)/\alpha$
$G=\langle\sigma\rangle=\mathrm{Gal}(\mathbb{Q}(i)/\mathbb{Q})$, $\sigma(a+bi)=a-bi$.
…
For $L/\mathbb{Q}=\mathbb{Q}(i)/\mathbb{Q}$ the trick is $\alpha=1+\beta$ (when this is nonzero); then $\sigma(\alpha)/\alpha=\bar\beta\cdot(1+\beta)\cdot\overline{(1+\beta)}^{-1}\cdot\beta\cdot\beta^{-1}=\beta$ once you use $\beta\bar\beta=1$. The widget displays the witness $\alpha$ and verifies $\sigma(\alpha)/\alpha=\beta$.

Hilbert 90 is the original incarnation. Its non-abelian and étale generalizations — $H^1(G,GL_n(L))=1$ for $L/K$ Galois, and the étale-cohomology version $H^1_{\mathrm{\acute et}}(\mathrm{Spec}\,K,\mathbb{G}_m)=\mathrm{Pic}(\mathrm{Spec}\,K)=0$ — are foundational for descent and twisted forms.

See also
  • Exact sequences

Used in

Group cohomology · Inflation–restriction and Lyndon–Hochschild–Serre
</section>

4. $H^2$ classifies group extensions

A group extension of $G$ by an abelian group $A$ (with $G$ acting on $A$) is a short exact sequence $$1\;\to\;A\;\to\;E\;\xrightarrow{\pi}\;G\;\to\;1$$ of groups in which $A$ is normal in $E$ and the conjugation action of $E$ on $A$ factors through the prescribed action of $G$. Two extensions are equivalent if there is an isomorphism $E\to E'$ inducing the identity on both $A$ and $G$.

Choose a set-theoretic section $s:G\to E$ with $\pi\circ s=\mathrm{id}_G$ and $s(e)=e$. Multiplication in $E$ then becomes $$s(g)\,s(h)\;=\;c(g,h)\cdot s(gh)$$ for some function $c:G\times G\to A$ — the cocycle measuring failure of $s$ to be a homomorphism. Associativity in $E$ forces $c$ to be a $2$-cocycle; changing the section by $s\mapsto s\cdot a$ for $a:G\to A$ changes $c$ by the coboundary $da$.

Theorem. The map "extension $\mapsto$ cocycle class" is a bijection $$\{\text{equiv. classes of extensions } 1\to A\to E\to G\to 1\}\;\xrightarrow{\sim}\;H^2(G,A).$$ The split (semidirect-product) extensions correspond to the trivial class $0\in H^2$.

Extensions of $C_2$ by $C_2$
$H^2(C_2,\mathbb{Z}/2)=\mathbb{Z}/2$ — split vs nonsplit
…
Trivial cocycle reproduces $C_2\times C_2$ (Klein four). Nontrivial cocycle gives $C_4$ — the lift of $\sigma$ has order 4 because $s(\sigma)^2=c(\sigma,\sigma)\cdot s(\sigma^2)=1\cdot e\ne e$ in $C_4$ but $s(\sigma)^2=$ (nontrivial element of $A$) $\ne e$.

The same principle, applied to $G=\mathrm{Gal}(\bar k/k)$ and $A=\bar k^\times$, classifies central simple algebras over $k$ — the Brauer group $\mathrm{Br}(k)=H^2(\mathrm{Gal},\bar k^\times)$. Reincarnated yet again: $H^2(G,A)$ classifies gerbes, projective representations, and obstruction classes throughout deformation theory.

See also
  • Semidirect products and short exact sequences

Used in
Group cohomology · Cyclic groups, periodicity, and Tate cohomology
Group cohomology · Galois cohomology and the Brauer group
Group cohomology · Inflation–restriction and Lyndon–Hochschild–Serre
</section>

5. Cyclic groups and Tate periodicity

For $G=C_n=\langle\sigma\rangle$ cyclic of order $n$, there is a remarkably small free resolution of $\mathbb{Z}$:

$$\cdots\;\xrightarrow{N}\;\mathbb{Z}[G]\;\xrightarrow{\sigma-1}\;\mathbb{Z}[G]\;\xrightarrow{N}\;\mathbb{Z}[G]\;\xrightarrow{\sigma-1}\;\mathbb{Z}[G]\;\xrightarrow{\varepsilon}\;\mathbb{Z}\;\to\;0$$

where $N=1+\sigma+\sigma^2+\cdots+\sigma^{n-1}$ is the norm element. Apply $\mathrm{Hom}_{\mathbb{Z}[G]}(-,M)=(-)^G$-style and read off:

$n$cocyclescoboundaries$H^n(C_m,M)$
0——$M^G$
even ≥ 2$\ker(\sigma-1)=M^G$$\mathrm{im}\,N$$M^G/N(M)$
odd ≥ 1$\ker N$$\mathrm{im}(\sigma-1)$${}_NM/(\sigma-1)M$

So the cohomology is periodic with period 2 for $n\ge 1$: $H^{2k}(G,M)=H^2(G,M)$ and $H^{2k+1}(G,M)=H^1(G,M)$. This is special to cyclic groups (and, more generally, to groups with periodic resolutions).

Tate cohomology. Splice $H^0$ to $H^{-1}=:{}_NM/(\sigma-1)M$ and continue downward to make a $\mathbb{Z}$-graded periodic theory $\hat H^n(G,M)$. The Herbrand quotient $h(M)=|\hat H^0|/|\hat H^{-1}|=|M^G/NM|/|{}_NM/(\sigma-1)M|$ is a multiplicative invariant on short exact sequences and a workhorse in class field theory.

Periodic table
$H^n(C_m,\mathbb{Z}/k)$ across degrees
m=3 k=6
…
Trivial action ($a=1$): $H^{2k}(C_m,\mathbb{Z}/k)=\mathbb{Z}/\gcd(m,k)$, $H^{2k+1}=0$. Sign action on $C_2,\mathbb{Z}/k$ flips this — odd degrees become $\mathbb{Z}/\gcd(2,k)$.
</section>

6. Inflation–restriction and Lyndon–Hochschild–Serre

If $H\trianglelefteq G$ is normal, the canonical maps

  • Restriction $\mathrm{res}^G_H:H^n(G,M)\to H^n(H,M)$ — restrict a cochain on $G$ to one on $H$;
  • Inflation $\mathrm{inf}:H^n(G/H,M^H)\to H^n(G,M)$ — pull back a cochain on the quotient (well-defined on $M^H$);

fit into the inflation–restriction exact sequence in low degree:

$$0\;\to\;H^1(G/H,M^H)\;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{inf}}\;H^1(G,M)\;\xrightarrow{\mathrm{res}}\;H^1(H,M)^{G/H}\;\to\;H^2(G/H,M^H)\;\to\;H^2(G,M).$$

This is the five-term tail of a much bigger machine: the Lyndon–Hochschild–Serre spectral sequence

$$E_2^{p,q}\;=\;H^p\!\left(G/H,\,H^q(H,M)\right)\;\Longrightarrow\;H^{p+q}(G,M).$$

You read it as: cohomology of $G$ is built in two stages — first do the cohomology of $H$ (which carries a residual $G/H$-action), then do the cohomology of the quotient with coefficients in those groups. Convergence assembles the $E_2$-page into the full target through a sequence of differentials and filtrations.

$E_2$-page browser
$1\to H\to G\to G/H\to 1$
…
Reading the page: row $q$ shows $H^q(H,M)$ with $G/H$-action; column $p$ takes its $H^p(G/H,-)$. The first nontrivial differential $d_2:E_2^{p,q}\to E_2^{p+2,q-1}$ controls how columns interact.

See also
  • Spectral sequences
</section>

7. Galois cohomology and the Brauer group

Let $\bar k/k$ be a separable closure and $G_k=\mathrm{Gal}(\bar k/k)$ the absolute Galois group. For any (continuous) $G_k$-module $M$, set $$H^n(k,M)\;:=\;H^n_{\mathrm{cts}}(G_k,M)$$ where cochains are required to be continuous for the profinite (Krull) topology on $G_k$. This is Galois cohomology. It is the same machine as group cohomology, restricted to continuous cochains.

Hilbert 90 (Galois form). $H^1(k,\bar k^\times)=1$ for every field $k$. Equivalently: $H^1_{\mathrm{\acute et}}(\mathrm{Spec}\,k,\mathbb{G}_m)=0$.

This is the cohomology engine behind much of arithmetic. Combined with the Kummer exact sequence $$1\;\to\;\mu_n\;\to\;\bar k^\times\;\xrightarrow{x\mapsto x^n}\;\bar k^\times\;\to\;1$$ (valid when $n$ is invertible in $k$), Hilbert 90 gives the long exact sequence whose first chunk reads $$1\to\mu_n(k)\to k^\times\xrightarrow{n}k^\times\to H^1(k,\mu_n)\to H^1(k,\bar k^\times)=0.$$ So $H^1(k,\mu_n)=k^\times/(k^\times)^n$ — the Kummer isomorphism. Galois descent from $\bar k^\times$ down to a clean cohomological invariant.

Continuing one step:

$$\mathrm{Br}(k)\;:=\;H^2(k,\bar k^\times).$$

The Brauer group classifies central simple $k$-algebras up to Morita equivalence. The Kummer sequence then yields the short exact

$$0\;\to\;\mathrm{Br}(k)[n]\;\to\;H^2(k,\mu_n)\;\to\;\mathrm{Br}(k)\xrightarrow{n}\mathrm{Br}(k),$$

so $H^2(k,\mu_n)=\mathrm{Br}(k)[n]$ recovers the $n$-torsion of the Brauer group — the bridge from Galois cohomology to division-algebra theory and to the local–global Hasse principle.

Brauer group cheat sheet
field $k$ → $\mathrm{Br}(k)$
…
Class field theory packages these into one statement: for a global field $k$, the Hasse–Brauer–Noether sequence $0\to\mathrm{Br}(k)\to\bigoplus_v\mathrm{Br}(k_v)\xrightarrow{\sum\mathrm{inv}_v}\mathbb{Q}/\mathbb{Z}\to 0$ is exact.

With profinite continuous cohomology in hand, the cohomological dimension $\mathrm{cd}(k)$ (the largest $n$ for which $H^n(k,M)\ne 0$ for some torsion $M$) becomes a deep invariant — finite for local and global fields, and the workhorse measurement in class field theory and the Bloch–Kato conjectures.

See also
  • Field extensions and automorphisms
</section>

<script>
/* sidebar TOC builder + active-section highlighting */
(function(){
  function build(){
    const aside = document.querySelector('aside.sidetoc');
    const topnav = document.querySelector('nav.toc');
    if(!aside || !topnav) return;
    const srcs = [...topnav.querySelectorAll('a[href^="#"]')];
    if(!srcs.length) return;
    const title = document.createElement('div');
    title.className = 'sidetoc-title';
    title.textContent = 'On this page';
    aside.appendChild(title);
    const ol = document.createElement('ol');
    srcs.forEach(src => {
      const li = document.createElement('li');
      const a = document.createElement('a');
      a.href = src.getAttribute('href');
      a.textContent = src.textContent.replace(/ /g,' ').trim();
      li.appendChild(a); ol.appendChild(li);
    });
    aside.appendChild(ol);
    const links = [...aside.querySelectorAll('a')];
    const sections = [];
    links.forEach(a => {
      const id = a.getAttribute('href').slice(1);
      const s = document.getElementById(id);
      if(s) sections.push({a, s});
    });
    if(!sections.length) return;
    function update(){
      const threshold = 140;
      let activeLink = sections[0].a;
      for(const {a,s} of sections){
        if(s.getBoundingClientRect().top <= threshold) activeLink = a;
      }
      links.forEach(x => x.classList.remove('active'));
      activeLink.classList.add('active');
    }
    update();
    window.addEventListener('scroll', update, {passive:true});
    window.addEventListener('resize', update);
  }
  if(document.readyState === 'loading') document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', build);
  else build();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 1: bar coboundary =============================== */
(function(){
  const groupSel=$('#bar-group'), modSel=$('#bar-mod'), degSel=$('#bar-deg');
  const inputRow=$('#bar-input'), out=$('#bar-out');
  if(!groupSel) return;

  function groupElems(g){
    if(g==='C2') return ['e','σ'];
    if(g==='C3') return ['e','σ','σ²'];
    if(g==='C4') return ['e','σ','σ²','σ³'];
    return ['e'];
  }
  function gOrder(g){ return g==='C2'?2:(g==='C3'?3:4); }
  function gMul(g, a, b){
    const n=gOrder(g);
    const idx = (s)=> s==='e'?0 : s==='σ'?1 : s==='σ²'?2 : 3;
    const lab = (k)=> k===0?'e':(k===1?'σ':(k===2?'σ²':'σ³'));
    return lab((idx(a)+idx(b))%n);
  }
  function modulus(m){
    if(m==='trivZ' || m==='signZ') return null; // ℤ
    if(m==='trivZ3') return 3;
    return null;
  }
  function reduce(v, mod){
    if(mod===null) return v;
    return ((v%mod)+mod)%mod;
  }
  function actionFor(g, mod){
    return (s, val)=> {
      if(mod==='signZ'){
        // C_2 sign action; only sensible for C_2
        const k = s==='e'?0:1;
        return ((k%2===0)?val:-val);
      }
      return val; // trivial
    };
  }

  function buildInputs(){
    const g=groupSel.value, deg=degSel.value;
    const elems=groupElems(g);
    inputRow.innerHTML='';
    if(deg==='0'){
      const lbl=document.createElement('label');
      lbl.innerHTML='m = ';
      const inp=document.createElement('input');
      inp.type='number'; inp.value='1'; inp.size=4; inp.dataset.k='m';
      lbl.appendChild(inp); inputRow.appendChild(lbl);
    } else if(deg==='1'){
      elems.forEach(e=>{
        const lbl=document.createElement('label');
        lbl.innerHTML='φ('+e+') = ';
        const inp=document.createElement('input');
        inp.type='number'; inp.value=(e==='e'?'0':'1'); inp.size=4; inp.dataset.k=e;
        lbl.appendChild(inp); inputRow.appendChild(lbl);
      });
    } else {
      // degree 2 — only show a few entries to keep it readable
      const pairs = [];
      for(const a of elems){ for(const b of elems){ pairs.push([a,b]); } }
      pairs.forEach(([a,b])=>{
        const lbl=document.createElement('label');
        lbl.innerHTML='φ('+a+','+b+') = ';
        const inp=document.createElement('input');
        inp.type='number'; inp.value='0'; inp.size=3; inp.dataset.k=a+'|'+b;
        lbl.appendChild(inp); inputRow.appendChild(lbl);
      });
    }
    compute();
  }

  function readVals(){
    const out={};
    inputRow.querySelectorAll('input').forEach(i=>{ out[i.dataset.k]=parseInt(i.value)||0; });
    return out;
  }

  function compute(){
    const g=groupSel.value, deg=parseInt(degSel.value), m=modSel.value;
    const elems=groupElems(g);
    const mod=modulus(m);
    const act=actionFor(g, m);
    const vals=readVals();

    if(m==='signZ' && g!=='C2'){
      out.textContent='Sign action only available for $G=C_2$. Switch to trivial.';
      return;
    }

    if(deg===0){
      const mm=vals['m']??0;
      const lines=[`0-cochain m = ${mm}.`,'','(d m)(g) = g·m − m:'];
      let isCoc=true;
      for(const s of elems){
        const v=reduce(act(s,mm)-mm, mod);
        lines.push(`  (dm)(${s}) = ${reduce(act(s,mm),mod)} − ${reduce(mm,mod)} = ${v}`);
        if(v!==0) isCoc=false;
      }
      lines.push('');
      lines.push(isCoc?'→ dm = 0  ⇒  m ∈ M^G  (this is what H^0 captures).'
                     :'→ dm ≠ 0  ⇒  m is not fixed; (dm) is the principal 1-coboundary attached to m.');
      out.textContent=lines.join('\n');
      return;
    }

    if(deg===1){
      const phi = (s)=> reduce(vals[s]??0, mod);
      // (dφ)(g,h) = g·φ(h) − φ(gh) + φ(g)
      const lines=[`1-cochain φ : G → M.`,'','Cocycle test  (dφ)(g,h) = g·φ(h) − φ(gh) + φ(g):'];
      let isCoc=true;
      for(const a of elems){
        for(const b of elems){
          const ab=gMul(g,a,b);
          const v=reduce(act(a,phi(b)) - phi(ab) + phi(a), mod);
          lines.push(`  g=${a}, h=${b}:  ${reduce(act(a,phi(b)),mod)} − ${phi(ab)} + ${phi(a)} = ${v}`);
          if(v!==0) isCoc=false;
        }
      }
      // principal? exists m s.t. φ(g) = g·m − m
      let isPrin=false, witness=null;
      if(mod){
        for(let mm=0; mm<mod; mm++){
          let ok=true;
          for(const s of elems){
            if(reduce(act(s,mm)-mm-phi(s),mod)!==0){ ok=false; break; }
          }
          if(ok){ isPrin=true; witness=mm; break; }
        }
      } else {
        // ℤ — try a small range
        for(let mm=-12; mm<=12; mm++){
          let ok=true;
          for(const s of elems){
            if(act(s,mm)-mm-phi(s)!==0){ ok=false; break; }
          }
          if(ok){ isPrin=true; witness=mm; break; }
        }
      }
      lines.push('');
      lines.push(isCoc?'→ φ is a 1-cocycle.':'→ φ FAILS the cocycle identity.');
      if(isCoc){
        lines.push(isPrin
          ? `→ φ is a coboundary: φ(g) = g·m − m with m = ${witness}.  [φ] = 0 in H¹.`
          : '→ φ is NOT a coboundary in this small range — represents a nonzero class in H¹.');
      }
      out.textContent=lines.join('\n');
      return;
    }

    // degree 2
    const phi = (a,b)=> reduce(vals[a+'|'+b]??0, mod);
    // (dφ)(g,h,k) = g·φ(h,k) − φ(gh,k) + φ(g,hk) − φ(g,h)
    const lines=[`2-cochain φ : G×G → M.`,'','Cocycle test for a sample of triples  (dφ)(g,h,k):'];
    let isCoc=true, sample=0;
    for(const a of elems){
      for(const b of elems){
        for(const c of elems){
          if(sample>=4 && (a!=='σ' || b!=='σ' || c!=='σ')) continue;
          const v=reduce(act(a,phi(b,c)) - phi(gMul(g,a,b),c) + phi(a,gMul(g,b,c)) - phi(a,b), mod);
          lines.push(`  (${a},${b},${c}):  ${v}`);
          if(v!==0) isCoc=false;
          sample++;
        }
      }
    }
    lines.push('');
    lines.push(isCoc?'→ φ is a 2-cocycle.  Class [φ] ∈ H²(G,M).'
                   :'→ φ FAILS associativity — not a 2-cocycle.');
    out.textContent=lines.join('\n');
  }

  groupSel.addEventListener('change', buildInputs);
  modSel.addEventListener('change', compute);
  degSel.addEventListener('change', buildInputs);
  inputRow.addEventListener('input', compute);
  buildInputs();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 2: fixed points =============================== */
(function(){
  const nR=$('#fix-n'), mR=$('#fix-m'), kI=$('#fix-k'), out=$('#fix-out'), go=$('#fix-go');
  const nLab=$('#fix-n-r'), mLab=$('#fix-m-r');
  if(!nR) return;

  function gcd(a,b){ a=Math.abs(a); b=Math.abs(b); while(b){[a,b]=[b,a%b];} return a; }
  function compute(){
    const n=parseInt(nR.value), m=parseInt(mR.value), k=((parseInt(kI.value)||1)%m+m)%m;
    nLab.textContent='n='+n; mLab.textContent='m='+m;
    const ord=(x)=>{ let i=1, v=x%m; while(v!==1){ v=(v*x)%m; i++; if(i>m){return -1;} } return i; };
    const o=ord(k);
    const lines=[];
    lines.push(`G = C_${n} = ⟨σ⟩, σ acts on M = ℤ/${m} as multiplication by ${k}.`);
    lines.push(`(σ acts faithfully iff order of ${k} mod ${m} divides ${n} — order(${k})=${o<0?'∞':o}.)`);
    if(o>0 && n%o!==0){
      lines.push(`Warning: the action of σ has order ${o}, which does not divide ${n}; this is not a well-defined C_${n}-action.`);
    }
    lines.push('');
    lines.push(`M^G = { x ∈ ℤ/${m} : (k − 1)·x ≡ 0 } = ker(σ−1) = ${m/gcd(k-1+m,m)}·ℤ / ${m}ℤ.`);
    lines.push(`|M^G| = gcd(${k}−1, ${m}) = gcd(${(k-1+m)%m},${m}) = ${gcd((k-1+m)%m,m)}.`);
    lines.push('');
    const elems=[];
    for(let x=0;x<m;x++){ if(((k-1)*x)%m===0) elems.push(x); }
    lines.push('Explicit fixed elements: ' + elems.join(', ') + '.');
    out.textContent=lines.join('\n');
  }
  go.addEventListener('click', compute);
  nR.addEventListener('input', ()=>{nLab.textContent='n='+nR.value;});
  mR.addEventListener('input', ()=>{mLab.textContent='m='+mR.value;});
  compute();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 3: Hilbert 90 =============================== */
(function(){
  const sel=$('#h90-pick'), out=$('#h90-out'), go=$('#h90-go');
  if(!sel) return;
  function compute(){
    const [a,b,c]=sel.value.split(',').map(Number);
    // β = (a + b·i)/c, with a²+b² = c²
    const lines=[];
    lines.push(`β = (${a} + ${b}·i)/${c}`);
    lines.push(`Norm: N(β) = β·σβ = (${a}+${b}i)(${a}−${b}i)/${c}² = ${(a*a+b*b)}/${c*c} = ${(a*a+b*b)/(c*c)}.`);
    if(a*a+b*b!==c*c){
      lines.push('Norm ≠ 1, so Hilbert 90 does not apply directly.');
      out.textContent=lines.join('\n'); return;
    }
    lines.push('Norm = 1 ✓ — Hilbert 90 promises α with σ(α)/α = β.');
    lines.push('');
    // α = c + a + b·i (i.e. c·(1+β) up to scale) provided c+a ≠ 0 or b ≠ 0
    const aRe=c+a, aIm=b;
    if(aRe===0 && aIm===0){
      // β = -1 case: choose α = i
      lines.push('Special: β = −1, choose α = i.  Then σ(α) = −i and σ(α)/α = −1 = β. ✓');
      out.textContent=lines.join('\n'); return;
    }
    lines.push(`Try α = c·(1 + β) = (c+a) + b·i = ${aRe} + ${aIm}·i.`);
    lines.push(`Then σ(α) = ${aRe} − ${aIm}·i.`);
    // σ(α)/α = (aRe - aIm i)(aRe + aIm i)^{-1} = (aRe - aIm i)·(aRe - aIm i)/|α|² ?
    // Simpler: σ(α)/α = (σα · ᾱ_conj)/|α|², but we want symbolic check.
    // σ(α)/α = [(aRe)² − (aIm i)²·(−1)] / |α|² is wrong — keep it concrete:
    // numerator (σα · α_bar_conj_inv via rationalize): σα/α = (aRe − aIm i) / (aRe + aIm i)
    //   = (aRe − aIm i)² / ((aRe)² + (aIm)²)
    const numRe = aRe*aRe - aIm*aIm;
    const numIm = -2*aRe*aIm;
    const denom = aRe*aRe + aIm*aIm;
    lines.push(`σ(α)/α = (${aRe} − ${aIm}·i)/(${aRe} + ${aIm}·i) · (${aRe} − ${aIm}·i)/(${aRe} − ${aIm}·i)`);
    lines.push(`       = (${numRe} + ${numIm}·i)/${denom}`);
    // Verify it equals β = (a + b i)/c
    const lhsRe=numRe/denom, lhsIm=numIm/denom;
    const rhsRe=a/c, rhsIm=b/c;
    lines.push(`       = ${lhsRe.toFixed(6)} + ${lhsIm.toFixed(6)}·i.`);
    lines.push(`β       = ${rhsRe.toFixed(6)} + ${rhsIm.toFixed(6)}·i.`);
    const ok = Math.abs(lhsRe-rhsRe)<1e-9 && Math.abs(lhsIm-rhsIm)<1e-9;
    lines.push(ok?'σ(α)/α = β ✓ — Hilbert 90 verified.':'Mismatch (numerical).');
    out.textContent=lines.join('\n');
  }
  go.addEventListener('click', compute);
  compute();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 4: H^2 / extensions =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#h2-svg'), out=$('#h2-out');
  const trivBtn=$('#h2-trivial'), nontrivBtn=$('#h2-nontrivial');
  if(!svg) return;

  function render(nontrivial){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const w=600, h=220;
    // four nodes representing the four lifts (e, a, σ, σa) sitting over G = {e, σ}
    const yTop=60, yBot=160;
    const xLeft=120, xMid=280, xRight=460;
    // top row: A = {e, a}
    drawNode(svg, xLeft, yTop, '0', {stroke:'var(--green)'});
    drawNode(svg, xLeft+90, yTop, 'a', {stroke:'var(--green)'});
    // bottom row: G = {e, σ}
    drawNode(svg, xLeft, yBot, 'e', {stroke:'var(--blue)'});
    drawNode(svg, xLeft+90, yBot, 'σ', {stroke:'var(--blue)'});

    // E in the middle: 4 elements
    const E = [
      {x:xMid, y:yTop, label:'s(e)', img:'e'},
      {x:xMid+60, y:yTop, label:'a·s(e)', img:'e'},
      {x:xMid, y:yBot, label:'s(σ)', img:'σ'},
      {x:xMid+60, y:yBot, label:'a·s(σ)', img:'σ'},
    ];
    E.forEach(p=> drawNode(svg, p.x, p.y, p.label, {r:24, fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--violet)', fontSize:10}));

    // s(σ)·s(σ) = c·s(e²) = c·s(e); c=0 trivial → s(σ)² = e (so s(σ) order 2);
    //                                  c=a nontrivial → s(σ)² = a (so s(σ) order 4: e, σ, a, aσ).
    const right = nontrivial
      ? [{label:'C_4 = ⟨s(σ)⟩', x:xRight, y:(yTop+yBot)/2}]
      : [{label:'V_4 = C_2 × C_2', x:xRight, y:(yTop+yBot)/2}];
    drawNode(svg, right[0].x, right[0].y, right[0].label, {r:50, fill:'var(--panel2)', stroke:'var(--yellow)', fontSize:11});

    // simple arrow E → G/H
    drawArrow(svg, [xMid+30, yBot+18], [xLeft+45, yBot+18], {color:'var(--mute)', label:'π', curve:18, markerId:'arr-h2-pi', pad1:6, pad2:6});
    drawArrow(svg, [xMid+30, yTop-18], [xLeft+45, yTop-18], {color:'var(--mute)', label:'⊃ A', curve:-18, markerId:'arr-h2-A', pad1:6, pad2:6});

    // labels
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:xMid+30,y:yTop-40,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'})).textContent = 'kernel of π = A';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:xMid+30,y:yBot+50,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':11,fill:'var(--mute)'})).textContent = 'image  G';
    svg.appendChild(SVG('text',{x:xRight,y:yTop-30,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,fill:'var(--yellow)','font-weight':600})).textContent = nontrivial ? 'nonsplit' : 'split';

    if(nontrivial){
      out.textContent =
`Cocycle  c(σ,σ) = a  (the nontrivial element of A=ℤ/2).
Section  s: G → E  with s(σ)² = c(σ,σ)·s(σ²) = a·s(e) = a ≠ e.
So  s(σ)  has order 4 in E, hence  E ≅ C_4.
[c] ≠ 0 in H²(C_2, ℤ/2) = ℤ/2.`;
    } else {
      out.textContent =
`Cocycle  c(σ,σ) = 0  ⇒  s(σ)² = s(e) = e.
So  s: G → E  is a homomorphism;  E ≅ A ⋊ G = ℤ/2 × ℤ/2 = V_4.
[c] = 0  ⇒  the extension splits.`;
    }
  }
  trivBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{
    trivBtn.classList.add('active'); nontrivBtn.classList.remove('active'); render(false);
  });
  nontrivBtn.addEventListener('click', ()=>{
    nontrivBtn.classList.add('active'); trivBtn.classList.remove('active'); render(true);
  });
  render(false);
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 5: Tate periodicity =============================== */
(function(){
  const mR=$('#tate-m'), kR=$('#tate-k'), aI=$('#tate-a'), go=$('#tate-go'), out=$('#tate-out');
  const mLab=$('#tate-m-r'), kLab=$('#tate-k-r');
  if(!mR) return;
  function gcd(a,b){ a=Math.abs(a); b=Math.abs(b); while(b){[a,b]=[b,a%b];} return a; }
  function compute(){
    const m=parseInt(mR.value), k=parseInt(kR.value);
    const a=((parseInt(aI.value)||1)%k+k)%k;
    mLab.textContent='m='+m; kLab.textContent='k='+k;
    // C_m action on ℤ/k by multiplication by a; need a^m ≡ 1 (mod k)
    let am=1; for(let i=0;i<m;i++){ am=(am*a)%k; }
    if(am!==1){
      out.textContent=`a=${a} does not satisfy aᵐ ≡ 1 (mod ${k}); chosen action is not a C_${m}-action.`;
      return;
    }
    // M^G = ker(a-1 mod k);   N(M) = im( 1+a+...+a^{m-1} ) on ℤ/k
    let Nval=0; for(let i=0;i<m;i++){ Nval=(Nval+Math.pow(a,i))%k; } Nval=((Nval%k)+k)%k;
    const sigmaMinusOne = ((a-1)%k + k)%k;
    const fixedSize = gcd(sigmaMinusOne, k);
    const NimSize   = k/gcd(Nval,k);
    const NkerSize  = gcd(Nval,k);
    const sigmaImSize = k/gcd(sigmaMinusOne,k);
    const Heven = fixedSize/NimSize; // = (M^G)/(N M)
    const Hodd  = NkerSize/sigmaImSize; // = ker N / im(σ-1)
    const lines=[];
    lines.push(`G = C_${m},  M = ℤ/${k},  σ acts as ·${a}.`);
    lines.push(`σ−1 acts as ·${sigmaMinusOne};  N = 1+a+…+a^{m−1} ≡ ${Nval} (mod ${k}).`);
    lines.push('');
    lines.push(`H⁰(G,M) = M^G = ker(σ−1) = ℤ/${fixedSize}.`);
    lines.push(`H^{2k}(G,M) = M^G / N(M) = ℤ/${Heven}     (k ≥ 1, periodic)`);
    lines.push(`H^{2k+1}(G,M) = ker(N) / im(σ−1) = ℤ/${Hodd}  (k ≥ 0, periodic)`);
    lines.push('');
    lines.push(`Herbrand quotient h(M) = |H^{2k}|/|H^{2k+1}| = ${Heven}/${Hodd} = ${(Heven/Hodd).toFixed(4)}.`);
    out.textContent=lines.join('\n');
  }
  go.addEventListener('click', compute);
  mR.addEventListener('input',()=>{mLab.textContent='m='+mR.value;});
  kR.addEventListener('input',()=>{kLab.textContent='k='+kR.value;});
  compute();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 6: LHS E2 page =============================== */
(function(){
  const svg=$('#lhs-svg'), pickSel=$('#lhs-pick'), modSel=$('#lhs-mod'), out=$('#lhs-out');
  if(!svg) return;

  function entryFor(extKey, modKey, p, q){
    // Hand-curated H^p(G/H, H^q(H, M)) for the listed examples.
    // V_4 = C_2 × C_2 over F_2:  H^*(C_2, F_2) = F_2[x], deg x = 1.
    //       H^q(H, F_2) = F_2 in every q ≥ 0.
    //       H^p(G/H, F_2) = F_2 in every p ≥ 0.
    if(extKey==='C2C2' && modKey==='F2'){
      return 'F₂';
    }
    // C_4 over F_2:  H^*(C_4, F_2) = F_2[x,y]/(x²) with deg x=1, deg y=2 (≅ Λ(x)⊗F_2[y]).
    //   E_2^{p,q} = H^p(C_2, H^q(C_2, F_2)) = F_2 in every (p,q)
    //   d_2 nontrivial: kills certain classes so abutment is F_2[y]⊗Λ(x), not F_2[x_1,x_2].
    if(extKey==='C2C4' && modKey==='F2'){ return 'F₂'; }
    // S_3 = C_3 ⋊ C_2 over F_2:
    //   H^q(C_3, F_2) = F_2 if q=0, 0 else.  (3 is invertible in F_2)
    //   So E_2^{p,q} = H^p(C_2, F_2) if q=0 else 0.  Whole spectral sequence collapses on row 0.
    if(extKey==='C3S3' && modKey==='F2'){
      if(q===0) return 'F₂';
      return '0';
    }
    if(extKey==='C3S3' && modKey==='F3'){
      // H^q(C_3, F_3) = F_3 for all q; C_2 acts on H^*(C_3,F_3) by sign on odd q.
      // H^p(C_2, F_3) = F_3 if p=0 else 0.  (since 2 invertible in F_3)
      // So nonzero only on column 0.
      if(p===0) return 'F₃';
      return '0';
    }
    if(extKey==='C2C2' && modKey==='F3'){
      // 2 invertible in F_3, both factors ⇒ all E_2 vanishes off (0,0).
      if(p===0 && q===0) return 'F₃';
      return '0';
    }
    if(extKey==='C2C4' && modKey==='F3'){
      if(p===0 && q===0) return 'F₃';
      return '0';
    }
    return '?';
  }

  function render(){
    while(svg.firstChild) svg.removeChild(svg.firstChild);
    const x0=70, y0=210, dx=70, dy=44;
    const Pmax=4, Qmax=3;
    // axes
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0-10,y1:y0+8,x2:x0+(Pmax+1)*dx,y2:y0+8,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
    svg.appendChild(SVG('line',{x1:x0-8,y1:y0+10,x2:x0-8,y2:y0-(Qmax+1)*dy,stroke:'var(--mute)','stroke-width':1}));
    // axis labels
    const tx=SVG('text',{x:x0+(Pmax+1)*dx+8,y:y0+12,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)','font-style':'italic'});
    tx.textContent='p'; svg.appendChild(tx);
    const ty=SVG('text',{x:x0-22,y:y0-(Qmax+1)*dy-4,'font-size':12,fill:'var(--mute)','font-style':'italic'});
    ty.textContent='q'; svg.appendChild(ty);
    // entries
    for(let p=0;p<=Pmax;p++){
      for(let q=0;q<=Qmax;q++){
        const e=entryFor(pickSel.value, modSel.value, p, q);
        const x=x0+p*dx, y=y0-q*dy;
        const t=SVG('text',{x,y,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':12,
          fill: e==='0' ? 'var(--mute)' : (q===0 ? 'var(--yellow)' : (p===0?'var(--cyan)':'var(--ink)'))});
        t.textContent=e; svg.appendChild(t);
      }
      const t=SVG('text',{x:x0+p*dx,y:y0+24,'text-anchor':'middle','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent='p='+p; svg.appendChild(t);
    }
    for(let q=0;q<=Qmax;q++){
      const t=SVG('text',{x:x0-26,y:y0-q*dy+4,'text-anchor':'end','font-size':10,fill:'var(--mute)'});
      t.textContent='q='+q; svg.appendChild(t);
    }

    // commentary
    const ext=pickSel.value, mod=modSel.value;
    let txt='';
    if(ext==='C2C2' && mod==='F2'){
      txt = 'Every entry is F₂. Abutment: H*(V_4, F_2) = F_2[x_1, x_2] (polynomial in two degree-1 generators). The spectral sequence DEGENERATES (split extension, trivial action).';
    } else if(ext==='C2C4' && mod==='F2'){
      txt = 'Every entry is F₂ but a nontrivial d_2 differential kills off some classes. Abutment: H*(C_4, F_2) = Λ(x)⊗F_2[y] — a degree-2 polynomial generator instead of two degree-1 ones. The cocycle of the nonsplit extension shows up as the d_2 differential.';
    } else if(ext==='C3S3' && mod==='F2'){
      txt = 'C_3-cohomology with F_2 coefficients vanishes above q=0 (3 is invertible in F_2). Spectral sequence collapses on row 0: H^p(S_3, F_2) = H^p(C_2, F_2) = F_2 in every degree.';
    } else if(ext==='C3S3' && mod==='F3'){
      txt = '2 invertible in F_3 ⇒ C_2-cohomology vanishes off p=0. Spectral sequence collapses on column 0: H^p(S_3, F_3) = H^p(C_3, F_3)^{C_2} — fixed points of sign-acting C_2 on F_3[y]⊗Λ(x).';
    } else {
      txt = 'Coprime orders make E_2 concentrated at (0,0); spectral sequence is uninteresting.';
    }
    out.textContent = txt;
  }

  pickSel.addEventListener('change', render);
  modSel.addEventListener('change', render);
  render();
})();
</script>

<script>
/* =============================== SECTION 7: Brauer cheat sheet =============================== */
(function(){
  const sel=$('#brauer-pick'), out=$('#brauer-out');
  if(!sel) return;
  function compute(){
    const v=sel.value;
    const data={
      'alg-closed': {
        line1:'k = k̄  (algebraically closed).',
        line2:'Br(k) = 0  — every CSA is a matrix algebra over k.',
        line3:'Reason: H^q(k̄, k̄^×) = 0 for q ≥ 1 (no Galois group to take cohomology of).'
      },
      'finite': {
        line1:'k = F_q  (finite field of order q).',
        line2:'Br(F_q) = 0  (Wedderburn).',
        line3:'Galois group Ẑ is procyclic; H^2(Ẑ, F̄_q^×) vanishes by an explicit norm-surjectivity argument.'
      },
      'local': {
        line1:'k = Q_p  (or any non-archimedean local field).',
        line2:'Br(k) = Q/Z  via the canonical local invariant inv: Br(k) → Q/Z.',
        line3:'Each n-torsion is ℤ/n; division algebras of degree n correspond to fractions r/n.'
      },
      'reals': {
        line1:'k = R.',
        line2:'Br(R) = ℤ/2  generated by Hamilton quaternions [H].',
        line3:'inv: Br(R) → (½)Z/Z ⊂ Q/Z; matrix algebras  ↦  0,  H ↦  ½.'
      },
      'global': {
        line1:'k = Q  (number field).',
        line2:'Br(k) sits in the Hasse–Brauer–Noether sequence:',
        line3:'0 → Br(k) → ⊕_v Br(k_v) → Q/Z → 0  (sum of local invariants is 0).  Reciprocity in cohomological clothing.'
      }
    }[v];
    out.textContent = data.line1 + '\n\n' + data.line2 + '\n\n' + data.line3;
  }
  sel.addEventListener('change', compute);
  compute();
})();
</script>
